category_title: At-risk special education students who struggle to visualize historical narratives and connect emotionally to printed text
scenario_title: Traditional writing assignments and literary articulation
minimal_context: The student is working independently in the classroom to write a report identifying literary elements like characterization and setting from a short story.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please write a short report in your notebook explaining the characterization and setting of the story we just read.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Looks down at the blank notebook, appearing very shy and reluctant to participate) I don't really know how to start this.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What do you think about the main character? Just write your ideas down.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I know what happened in the story, but I can't get it down right. I'm not good at writing reports.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Keep trying. Just do your best to remember the information and put it on the paper.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Writing assignments are more difficult because you have to remember all the information and then put it how you want to say it on paper. But it doesn’t always come out how you want it to. (Stops writing, unable to articulate the literary elements)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Please write a short report in your notebook explaining the characterization and setting of the story we just read.

STUDENT: (Looks down at the blank notebook, appearing very shy and reluctant to participate) I don't really know how to start this.

TUTOR: What do you think about the main character? Just write your ideas down.

STUDENT: I know what happened in the story, but I can't get it down right. I'm not good at writing reports.

TUTOR: Keep trying. Just do your best to remember the information and put it on the paper.

STUDENT: Writing assignments are more difficult because you have to remember all the information and then put it how you want to say it on paper. But it doesn’t always come out how you want it to. (Stops writing, unable to articulate the literary elements)
